title: Confidence-Building Patterns

anti_gaming_validation.notes: All tests are authentic with real assertions, no skip decorators, no placeholders

observations.note: Unit tests (21 tests) comprehensively cover all 4 acceptance criteria with section existence, principle validation, and NFR checks
Integration tests (13 tests) validate cross-file interactions: reference file paths, pattern consistency (validate-then-redirect), template completeness, and subagent execution contract
Verified that business-coach.md correctly references both confidence-building-patterns.md and imposter-syndrome-interventions.md with correct filenames for loading
Core principle 'validate then redirect' is consistently documented across both reference files and referenced in business-coach decision tree

Evidence-based affirmation templates contain all required placeholders: {milestone_count} (3 occurrences), {active_weeks} (1 occurrence), {completed_count} (2 occurrences). Total 7 placeholders across both reference files

Data Sources section (under Evidence-Based Affirmation) documents where to pull progress data: milestone completion count/dates, task completion velocity, revenue/customer metrics, skills acquired, duration of sustained effort
Momentum Tracking section references milestone achievements with {milestone_count} placeholder, ensuring consistency with evidence-based affirmation template data

Business-coach decision tree properly maps confidence detection to reference files: imposter syndrome signals → imposter-syndrome-interventions.md, general confidence concerns → confidence-building-patterns.md
Affirmation templates use consistent {placeholder_name} syntax enabling subagent to substitute user-specific data. Pattern verified in 7 total placeholders across the file
NFR-001 compliance verified: confidence-building-patterns.md (102 lines), imposter-syndrome-interventions.md (82 lines) — both well under 1500 line limit

ac1_confidence_patterns.notes: File exists, contains all 4 required sections, under 1500 lines

ac2_imposter_syndrome.notes: File exists, contains recognition triggers and validate-then-redirect sections, principle documented

ac3_subagent_confidence.notes: business-coach.md has confidence detection, references both files, includes technique application instructions

ac4_evidence_affirmation.notes: Templates contain affirmation examples with milestone data, progress placeholders, and concrete evidence references

test_categories.description: Validates file references are correct and files exist at expected paths
Ensures validate-then-redirect principle is consistent across files
Verifies all affirmation placeholders are documented with data sources
Validates business-coach can execute coaching workflow via reference files
Confirms reference files stay under 1500 line limit

recommendations.note: Consider adding integration test for coaching subagent loading and instantiating templates at runtime (would require test framework for subagent execution)
Add examples of affirmation templates with actual user data substitutions (e.g., 'You have completed 5 milestones...' instead of template syntax)

All ACs fully tested and passing. Consider adding e2e test for complete coaching workflow (detect confidence issue → load reference → apply intervention) once test infrastructure supports subagent invocation

conclusion: STORY-469 integration testing PASSED. All 34 tests (21 unit + 13 integration) pass successfully. Cross-file interactions validated: business-coach.md correctly references both reference files, pattern consistency verified across components, template completeness confirmed with all data placeholders present, and subagent execution contract validated. Story is ready for QA approval.
